sections.title: Three main problem areas (1)
sections.content: Word order in English often causes mistakes. This topic focuses on **three** areas:

sections.title: Three main problem areas (2)
sections.content:
1. **Embedded questions** – keeping **statement** word order (no inversion) after *I don't know*, *Can you tell me*, etc.
   Wrong: *I don't know what is the answer.* → correct: *I don't know what the answer is.*
2. **Frequency adverbs** – where to put *often*, *usually*, *always*, *sometimes* (mid-position in English).

sections.title: Three main problem areas (2)
sections.content:
Wrong: *I go often to the gym.* → correct: *I often go to the gym.*
3. **Additive particles** – where to put *also*, and the difference from *too* / *as well*.
   Wrong: *Also I want coffee.* → correct: *I also want coffee.*

sections.title: Embedded questions: no inversion (1)
sections.content:
In a **direct question** we put the verb before the subject:

• What **is** the answer? Where **is** the station?

When the question is **inside** another sentence (embedded), we use **statement** order – subject before verb:

sections.title: Embedded questions: no inversion (2)
sections.content:
• I don't know what **the answer is**. ✓  Wrong: *I don't know what is the answer.* ✗
• Can you tell me where **the station is**? ✓  Wrong: *Can you tell me where is the station?* ✗

**Wrong:** I don't know what *is the answer*.
**Right:** I don't know what **the answer is**.

sections.title: Embedded questions: when to use statement order (1)
sections.content:
Use **statement order** (subject + verb) after *I don't know*, *Can you tell me*, *I wonder*, *Tell me*, *Do you know*, *I'm not sure*.

• I don't know what **the answer is**. ✓  Wrong: *I don't know what is the answer.* ✗
• I wonder when **he left**. ✓  Wrong: *I wonder when did he leave.* ✗ (no *did*)
• Do you know **if she is** coming? ✓  Wrong: *Do you know if is she coming?* ✗

sections.title: Embedded questions: when to use statement order (2)
sections.content: **Rule:** Subject before verb. Past: when **he left** (not *when did he leave*). Present: how much **it costs** (not *does it cost*).

items.label: Example:
items.formula: What **is** the answer? → I don't know what the answer **is**.

sections.title: Frequency adverbs: where they go (1)
sections.content: In **English**, frequency adverbs (*often*, *usually*, *always*, *sometimes*) usually go in **mid-position**: after the first auxiliary, or before the main verb.

sections.title: Frequency adverbs: where they go (1)
sections.content:
• I **always** go. ✓  Wrong: *I go always.* / *Always I go.* ✗
• She **often** eats there. ✓  Wrong: *She eats often there.* ✗
• He **usually** works from home. ✓  Wrong: *He works usually from home.* ✗
• I **sometimes** forget. ✓  Wrong: *I forget sometimes.* ✗
• With **be**: She **is always** late. ✓  Wrong: *She always is late.* ✗

sections.title: Frequency adverbs: where they go (2)
sections.content:
• They **are usually** busy. ✓  Wrong: *They usually are busy.* ✗
• With one verb: adverb **before** the main verb (I often go). Wrong: *I go often.* ✗
• With auxiliary: adverb **after** the auxiliary (She doesn't **often** eat out. He has **always** been kind). ✓

sections.title: Frequency adverbs: common mistakes (1)
sections.content:
**Wrong:** I go *often* to the gym.
**Right:** I **often go** to the gym.

**Wrong:** She *always is* late.
**Right:** She **is always** late. (After **be**, the adverb goes after *am/is/are*.)

sections.title: Frequency adverbs: common mistakes (2)
sections.content:
**Wrong:** They *usually are* at home.
**Right:** They **are usually** at home.

**Rule of thumb:** With **be** → subject + be + adverb. With other verbs → subject + adverb + verb (or after first auxiliary in negatives/questions).

items.label: Other verbs:
items.formula: subject + adverb + **verb** (I **often go** to the gym)

items.label: With auxiliary:
items.formula: auxiliary + adverb + verb (She doesn't **often** eat out)

sections.title: Additive particles: "also" (1)
sections.content: **Also** is often put in the wrong place. In **English**, **also** usually goes in **mid-position**: before the main verb, or after the first auxiliary.

sections.title: Additive particles: "also" (1)
sections.content:
• I **also** want coffee. ✓  Wrong: *Also I want coffee.* / *I want also coffee.* ✗
• She **also** said that. ✓  Wrong: *Also she said that.* / *She said also that.* ✗
• We **also** need milk. ✓  Wrong: *Also we need milk.* ✗
• With auxiliary: He has **also** been there. ✓  Wrong: *He also has been there.* ✗
• She will **also** join us. ✓  Wrong: *Also she will join us.* ✗

sections.title: Additive particles: "also" (2)
sections.content:
**Wrong:** *Also I want* coffee. *I want also* coffee.
**Right:** I **also** want coffee.

sections.title: "Also" vs "too" and "as well" (1)
sections.content:
**Also** → mid-position:
• I **also** need a pen. ✓  Czenglish: *Also I need a pen.* / *I need also a pen.* ✗
• She has **also** finished. ✓  Czenglish: *Also she has finished.* ✗

**Too** and **as well** → usually at the **end** of the clause:

sections.title: "Also" vs "too" and "as well" (2)
sections.content:
• I need a pen **too**. ✓  Czenglish: *I need also a pen.* (use *too* at the end)
• She has finished **as well**. ✓
• I **also** want tea. Or: I want tea **too** / I want tea **as well**. ✓

So: **also** in the middle; **too** / **as well** at the end. Czenglish: *I want coffee also* → better: *I want coffee too*.

sections.title: Summary (1)
sections.content:
• **Embedded questions:** Statement order – what **the answer is**, when **he left**, how much **it costs**, if **she is** coming.
   Wrong: *I don't know what is the problem.* *I wonder when did he leave.* → use statement order.

sections.title: Summary (1)
sections.content:
• **Frequency adverbs** (often, usually, always, sometimes): mid-position – I often go; She is always late.
   Wrong: *I go often.* *She always is late.* *They usually are busy.* → adverb after *be*, before other verbs.

sections.title: Summary (2)
sections.content:
• **Also:** mid-position (I also want). **Too** / **as well:** end position (I want it too).
   Wrong: *Also I want coffee.* *I want also coffee.* → *I also want coffee* or *I want coffee too*.

questions.question: Which is correct?

options.a: I don't know what the answer is.
options.b: I don't know what is the answer.
options.c: I don't know what the answer.
options.d: I don't know what does the answer.

questions.explanation: Embedded question: use statement word order (subject + verb). What the answer is.

questions.question: 'Can you tell me _____?' (Where is the station?) Which fits?
questions.explanation: Embedded: where the station is (statement order).

questions.question: 'I wonder _____.' (When did he leave?) Which fits?
questions.explanation: Embedded: when he left (statement order; no auxiliary 'did').

questions.question: Which is correct?
questions.explanation: With the verb **be**, the frequency adverb goes **after** be: is always.

questions.question: Which is correct?

options.a: I go often to the gym.
options.b: I often go to the gym.
options.c: I go to the gym often.
options.d: Often I go to the gym.

questions.explanation: Frequency adverb in mid-position: before the main verb (I often go).

questions.question: Which is correct?
questions.explanation: **Also** usually goes in mid-position: before the main verb (I also want).

questions.question: Which is correct?

options.a: I don't know what is the answer.
options.b: I don't know what the answer is.
options.c: I don't know what the answer.
options.d: I don't know what does the answer.

questions.explanation: Embedded: what the answer is (statement order).

questions.question: Which is correct?

options.a: Can you tell me where is the station?
options.b: Can you tell me where the station is?
options.c: Can you tell me where the station?
options.d: Can you tell me where does the station?

questions.explanation: Embedded: where the station is.

questions.question: Which is correct?
questions.explanation: With be: adverb after is (is always).

questions.question: Which is correct?
questions.explanation: Frequency adverb before main verb: often goes.

questions.question: Which is correct?
questions.explanation: Also in mid-position: I also need.

questions.question: Which is correct?

options.a: I wonder when did he leave.
options.d: I wonder when he did leave.

questions.explanation: Embedded: when he left (no did).

questions.question: Which is correct?
questions.explanation: With be: are usually (adverb after auxiliary).

questions.question: Which is correct?

options.a: Do you know what is his name?
options.b: Do you know what his name is?
options.c: Do you know what his name?
options.d: Do you know what does his name?

questions.explanation: Embedded: what his name is.

questions.question: Which is correct?
questions.explanation: With be: am sometimes (adverb after be).

questions.question: Which is correct?
questions.explanation: Also after first auxiliary: has also finished. (Or: She has finished too.)

questions.question: I don't know what _____ (the answer / be). Use statement order.
questions.explanation: Embedded: what the answer is (subject + verb).

questions.question: Can you tell me where _____ (the station / be)? Use statement order.
questions.explanation: Embedded: where the station is.

questions.question: Do you know what _____ (his name / be)? Use statement order.
questions.explanation: Embedded: what his name is.

questions.question: I wonder when _____ (he / leave). Use statement order.
questions.explanation: Embedded: when he left (no 'did').

questions.question: Tell me how much _____ (it / cost). Use statement order.
questions.explanation: Embedded: how much it costs.

questions.question: I'm not sure what _____ (the problem / be). Use statement order.
questions.explanation: Embedded: what the problem is.

questions.question: Do you know where _____ (she / live)? Use statement order.
questions.explanation: Embedded: where she lives.

questions.question: I don't know if _____ (she / be) coming. Use statement order.
questions.explanation: Yes/No embedded: if she is coming.

questions.question: Can you tell me what time _____ (it / be)? Use statement order.
questions.explanation: Embedded: what time it is.

questions.question: I wonder why _____ (he / leave). Use statement order.
questions.explanation: Embedded: why he left.

questions.question: Wrong: 'I don't know what is the answer.' Correct the sentence.
answers: I don't know what the answer is.
I do not know what the answer is.
I don't know what the answer is
I do not know what the answer is
questions.explanation: Embedded question: statement order (what the answer is).

questions.question: Wrong: 'Can you tell me where is the station?' Correct the sentence.
answers: Can you tell me where the station is?
Can you tell me where the station is
questions.explanation: Embedded: where the station is.

questions.question: Wrong: 'I wonder when did he leave.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Embedded: when he left (no 'did').

questions.question: Wrong: 'She always is late.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: With be: adverb after be (is always).

questions.question: Wrong: 'I go often to the gym.' Correct the sentence.
answers: I often go to the gym.
I often go to the gym
questions.explanation: Frequency adverb before main verb: often go.

questions.question: Wrong: 'Also I want coffee.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Also in mid-position: I also want.

questions.question: Wrong: 'Do you know what is his name?' Correct the sentence.
answers: Do you know what his name is?
Do you know what his name is
questions.explanation: Embedded: what his name is.

questions.question: Wrong: 'Tell me how much does it cost.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Tell me how much it costs.
Tell me how much it costs
questions.explanation: Embedded: how much it costs.

questions.question: Wrong: 'They usually are at home.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: With be: are usually.

questions.question: Wrong: 'I don't know if is she coming?' Correct the sentence.
answers: Do you know if she is coming?
I don't know if she is coming.
Do you know if she's coming?
I don't know if she's coming.
Do you know if she is coming
I don't know if she is coming
Do you know if she's coming
I don't know if she's coming
questions.explanation: Yes/No embedded: if she is coming.
